macro_segments.title: The Location of the Daily Offering: Morning Northwest, Afternoon Northeast

micro_segments.title: Binding the animal's legs and Rambam's interpretation
The position of the rings: Northeast and Northwest corners of the altar
The reason for the positioning: facing the sun and avoiding shadows

macro_segments.title: Alexander the Great and the Ten Questions of the Sages of the South

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Ten things Alexander asked the southern elders
Which is greater distance: heaven to earth or east to west?
The sages' answer and why east to west is greater
Apparent brightness of sun at noon and the philosophical explanation
Light and darkness: Which was created first?
The sages' strategic refusal to answer about light and darkness
Who is wise, strong, wealthy, and how to live?
The sages' answers: anticipating the future, conquering the inclination, contentment
How to be accepted by people and the question of authority
Alexander's counter-answer: love kingship to help people
Ocean versus dry land: where is it better to live?
Which of you is smartest? The sages claim equality
Why do you oppose me? The Satan has won
Alexander's threat and the sages' appeal to his honor
Alexander rewards the sages with royal garments and gold necklaces
Thematic overview: Alexander's vaunting ambition and the sages' counter-philosophy
Parallels in classical literature: Plutarch's account of Alexander and the Gymnosophists

macro_segments.title: Alexander's Journey to Africa and Encounter with the Amazons

micro_segments.title: Alexander's desire to travel to Africa and the mountains of darkness
Instructions: Libyan donkeys, ropes, and crossing the mountains of darkness

macro_segments.title: Alexander's Journey to Africa and Encounter with the Amazons (continued)

micro_segments.title: Arrival at a city of women and the no-win situation of war
Gold bread and the futility of wealth and conquest
Alexander writes on the gates: 'I was a fool until I came to Africa'

macro_segments.title: Alexander at the Garden of Eden: The Eyeball and the Limit of Desire

micro_segments.title: Discovery of the fountain: salty fish in the well and the scent of Eden
Following the stream to the gates of the Garden of Eden
The gate is only for the righteous; Alexander receives an eyeball as a gift
The eyeball outweighs all Alexander's gold and silver
The sages explain: the eye is never satisfied; covering with dirt reveals the truth of death
Verse: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied, nor the eyes of man

macro_segments.title: Concluding Teachings on Torah Study and Peace

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiyya: Whoever learns Torah at night has the Shechinah opposite him
Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah: Disciples of the wise increase peace in the world
Verse interpretation: Your children learned from God will have great peace
This is the final Gemara of Seder Kodshim; Mishnayot follow

macro_segments.title: The Morning Service in the Temple: Blessing Before the Offering

micro_segments.title: The animals are brought halfway up the ramp and one blessing is made
The priests go to the chamber of hewn stone to recite Shema
Abbreviated service: blessing without Yotzer HaMe'orot before sunrise
Reading the Ten Commandments and the three paragraphs of Shema
Three blessings to the people: Emet VeYatziv, the service blessing, and blessing of the priests
On Shabbat, the departing group blesses the incoming group with unity and peace

topical_tags.term: Binding the legs (Rambam vs. other authorities)
Ring positions on the altar (northeast, northwest)
Heaven and earth distance versus east and west
Definitions of wisdom, strength, wealth, and life
The Amazons (city of women in Africa)
Garden of Eden (Gan Eden) and the eyeball
Torah study at night (Talmud Torah balaila)
Peace and disciples of the wise
